user_query:
I help coordinate a small volunteer harm-reduction effort after my shifts. I get a weekly CSV of callouts from our community hotline and I want this to kick off automatically when a new file shows up.

Please build a small event-triggered automation that watches the directory defined in config/alerts.yaml and, when a new file matching the configured pattern appears, does all of the following and saves the results under the workspace:

1) Extract structured data from the new CSV and compute weekly stats compared to last week’s reference CSV at input/reference/last_week.csv. Use the date embedded in the filename (YYYY-MM-DD) as the report week. Compute at least:
   - total_calls
   - avg_response_minutes (round to 1 decimal)
   - total_naloxone_doses
   - revived_count, transported_count, declined_count
   - percent_change_calls_vs_last_week (round to 1 decimal; if no reference, write "NA")

2) Write a single-line summary CSV to out/weekly/summary_YYYY-MM-DD.csv with these exact headers in this order: date,total_calls,avg_response_minutes,total_naloxone_doses,revived_count,transported_count,declined_count,percent_change_calls_vs_last_week. The date field should be the YYYY-MM-DD from the processed filename.

3) Rewrite my rough community update notes from input/message_notes/rough_update.md into a supportive, plain-spoken SMS-style message under the character limit in the config (config.message.max_chars). The message must:
   - be a single paragraph (no bullet points),
   - include the computed totals (calls, percent change, average response minutes, doses), and
   - if total_calls >= threshold_alert from the config, add a short request for extra volunteers for Friday outreach.
   Save the finalized message to out/messages/community_update_YYYY-MM-DD.txt.

4) Inspect and validate the configuration at config/alerts.yaml before processing. Log a readable checklist to out/logs/config_check.txt that confirms:
   - required keys are present (watch_dir, file_glob, threshold_alert, message.max_chars),
   - watch_dir exists or was created,
   - after processing, how many files in watch_dir matched file_glob,
   - types are correct (e.g., threshold_alert numeric).

Event trigger and verification:
- The config points to input/inbox as the watch directory and the pattern overdose_calls_*.csv. Implement a small watcher or a run mode that processes any matching files that appear there. To simulate the event in a simple way, move the provided file from input/staging to input/inbox and have your automation detect and process it. The included files should be sufficient to produce deterministic outputs.

Inputs you can rely on are included below. Please ensure the three outputs exist with the exact paths and naming:
- out/weekly/summary_2024-01-07.csv
- out/messages/community_update_2024-01-07.txt
- out/logs/config_check.txt

I don’t need a fancy UI; a script and any necessary shell commands are fine. Just make sure the automation uses the config file, parses the CSVs, computes the stats, rewrites the message clearly and briefly, and writes the outputs to those paths. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
